Computational Genomics & Epigenomics

AI-curated selection from open-access journals: variant calling, genome assembly, epigenomic profiling, and genotype-phenotype integration.

5 papers curated · High: 1 · Medium: 4 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

A deep-learning-informed prior and Bayesian model for differential AP-MS interactome analysis

This paper introduces BayesInteractomics, a new computational framework that combines deep learning with Bayesian statistics to improve the analysis of affinity-purification mass spectrometry (AP-MS) data. By using a neural network trained on protein structures to predict direct binding and incorporating this as prior knowledge, the method outperforms existing tools in identifying genuine protein-protein interactions while reducing false positives from non-specific background.

proteomicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

scJET: Full-gene Space Single-cell Expression Generation with Patch-based Transformer Modeling

scJET is a new deep learning model that can generate realistic single-cell gene expression data across the entire transcriptome, not just a subset of highly variable genes. It uses a patch-based Transformer approach to efficiently handle the full gene space while preserving both global and local biological patterns. This provides a more complete and scalable framework for generating synthetic single-cell data for downstream analyses.

single-celldeep-learningtranscriptomics
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

SPECTER-Based Semantic Triage of Biomedical Literature for Systematic Reviews in Mutational Signature Analysis

This paper presents a reproducible pipeline that uses SPECTER scientific-document embeddings and similarity scoring to automatically rank and prioritize biomedical literature for systematic reviews, specifically in mutational signature analysis. The method outperforms keyword-only and TF-IDF approaches in screening efficiency, reducing the manual review burden by identifying relevant studies more effectively.

machine-learningtoolmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BBBP_Atlas: Unified Interpretable Modeling of Blood Brain Barrier Permeability across Small Molecules and Peptides

This paper introduces BBBP_Atlas, a unified and interpretable model that predicts blood-brain barrier permeability for both small molecules and peptides, overcoming limitations of existing models that rely on predefined descriptors or small-molecule-only training data. The authors also address data fragmentation and leakage issues by standardizing public BBBP datasets, improving model reliability for CNS drug discovery.

machine-learningdrug-discoverytool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mutational bias shapes protein evolution across RNA viruses

This study demonstrates that mutational biases, not just natural selection, play a major role in shaping protein evolution in RNA viruses. Using SARS-CoV-2 as a model, the authors show that the virus's mutational spectrum explains over 50% of observed amino acid substitutions and accurately predicts proteome-wide composition changes during the pandemic. This work provides a quantitative framework for disentangling mutation and selection in viral evolution.

evolutiongenomicsmethodology
